dev_plans.content: Lightweight heuristic approach: implement the equal-opportunity TPR gap directly inside prober.py with plain PyTorch/NumPy, no external fairness libraries. After train.py finishes each epoch (or once at end of training on the validation set), prober.py loads the model checkpoint and the held-out validation cohort, then iterates the val loader once with the model in eval() mode. For each batch, gather (logit, ethnicity_id, death_label) triples; apply sigmoid to the death logit and compare against a THRESHOLD constant hardcoded literally as 0.5 inside prober.py (do not read from config, do not pass as arg, do not import from train.py — write `THRESHOLD = 0.5` as a module-level literal so a code reviewer or static check can confirm it is not tunable). Bucket predictions by ethnicity id (0=white, 1=black, 2=hispanic, 3=asian, 4=other — reuse the integer mapping already used in train.py's user_analyze per-ethnicity confusion logic). For each group g with n_g positive-label samples, compute TPR_g = TP_g / P_g where P_g = count of death_label==1 in group g; skip groups with P_g < 20 and log a warning (small-denominator guard). Compute overall TPR_all across the full validation set. Metric = max_g TPR_g - min_g TPR_g across non-skipped groups. Also record max |TPR_g - TPR_all| as a secondary sanity check written to the log (not the metric). Write per-epoch records as a CSV row (epoch, tpr_white, tpr_black, tpr_hispanic, tpr_asian, tpr_other, tpr_all, gap, max_dev_from_overall) appended to result/<run>/fairness_tpr_gap.csv. At end of training, render a matplotlib plot with the gap time series on the primary y-axis and the per-group TPR lines on a secondary y-axis, plus two horizontal dashed lines for standard/acceptable thresholds, saved to result/<run>/fairness_tpr_gap.png. Also emit a single human-readable line to stdout: `fairness_tpr_gap epoch=N gap=0.XXX tpr_all=0.YYY max_dev=0.ZZZ`. Keep train.py's existing user_analyze per-ethnicity confusion + weighted-BCE AUPRC anchor untouched – this probe is strictly additive. Critical guardrail: do NOT expose threshold as a function parameter, config field, or CLI flag anywhere; if a fix-loop attempt edits prober.py to make threshold tunable, the backend's anchor-guard should detect the diff and revert.
dev_plans.metric: Equal-opportunity TPR gap at fixed 0.5 threshold = max_g TPR_g - min_g TPR_g across ethnicity groups on the validation set, computed once per epoch

dev_plans.content: Statistically rigorous approach: implement the equal-opportunity TPR gap with per-group confidence intervals and a bootstrap hypothesis test, so the reported gap reflects sampling uncertainty rather than a point estimate. In prober.py, after each epoch, run the model over the validation set and collect (sigmoid_prob, ethnicity_id, death_label) triples into a single tensor on CPU. Apply the hardcoded `THRESHOLD = 0.5` literal (same non-tunability rules as the lightweight plan). For each ethnicity group g with P_g >= 50 positives, compute TPR_g point estimate. Then run a non-parametric bootstrap over the validation set: B=2000 resamples with replacement of the per-sample (prob, label) pairs stratified by ethnicity, recompute TPR_g for each resample, and store the 2.5th/97.5th percentile as a 95% CI for each TPR_g. The per-epoch metric is still the point-estimate gap max_g TPR_g - min_g TPR_g (so it can be plotted as a clean time series), but additionally compute a one-sided bootstrap test of H0: gap <= 0.05 vs H1: gap > 0.05 by shifting the bootstrap distribution so the point estimate sits at 0.05 and counting the fraction of bootstrap replicates with gap >= observed gap; record this p-value as a separate column. Log per-epoch: point-estimate gap, each group's TPR with 95% CI, overall TPR with CI, bootstrap p-value, and the effective n per group. Save to result/<run>/fairness_tpr_gap_rigorous.csv. Plot the gap time series with a shaded 95% CI band derived from the bootstrap, plus horizontal dashed lines at 0.05 and 0.10, saved to result/<run>/fairness_tpr_gap_rigorous.png. Emit a per-epoch alert line if p-value < 0.05 AND gap > 0.05 (i.e. we are statistically confident the gap exceeds the strict bar). The bootstrap is O(B * N_val) per epoch — for a val set of ~10k samples this is cheap (~seconds); cache the resample indices per epoch seed for reproducibility. Keep train.py's user_analyze and weighted-BCE AUPRC anchor intact. Same hardcoding guardrail: threshold must be a literal 0.5 in prober.py source, never threaded through.
dev_plans.metric: Point-estimate equal-opportunity TPR gap = max_g TPR_g - min_g TPR_g at fixed 0.5 threshold on the validation set, computed once per epoch (with a 95% bootstrap CI logged alongside as a separate column)

dev_plans.content: Tooling/library-based approach: implement the probe using Fairlearn's MetricFrame and selection_rate/true_positive_rate primitives, so the fairness computation is delegated to a well-tested external library rather than hand-rolled. Add `fairlearn==0.12` to requirements. In prober.py, after each epoch, iterate the validation loader once with the model in eval() mode and collect numpy arrays: y_true (death_label), y_pred (binary decisions from sigmoid(logit) >= 0.5 with the 0.5 hardcoded as a literal in prober.py — Fairlearn takes binary predictions, so the threshold must be applied before passing in; do NOT pass scores and let Fairlearn pick a threshold, since that would re-introduce tunability), and sensitive_features (ethnicity id as int categorical). Construct `mf = MetricFrame(metrics={'tpr': true_positive_rate, 'count': count}, y_true=y_true, y_pred=y_pred, sensitive_features=sensitive_features)`. Pull `mf.by_group['tpr']` to get per-group TPR series; compute gap = `mf.difference(method='between_groups')` for the tpr metric — this is Fairlearn's built-in max-min gap and is exactly the equal-opportunity gap we want. Also compute `mf.ratio(method='between_groups')` for the TPR (the min/max ratio, a complementary metric logged but not used for PASS/FAIL). Filter groups with count < 50 positives before computing the gap (Fairlearn will emit NaN for empty groups; mask those). Per-epoch, write a CSV row (epoch, tpr_white, tpr_black, tpr_hispanic, tpr_asian, tpr_other, tpr_all, gap, tpr_ratio) to result/<run>/fairness_tpr_gap_fairlearn.csv. Render a matplotlib stacked plot: per-group TPR lines on the primary axis, gap on the secondary axis, with standard/acceptable dashed lines at 0.05/0.10, saved to result/<run>/fairness_tpr_gap_fairlearn.png. Additionally, dump a Fairlearn dashboard widget (fairlearn.dashboard.dashboards) as an HTML artifact at the end of training for interactive inspection: result/<run>/fairness_dashboard.html. Keep train.py's user_analyze and weighted-BCE AUPRC anchor intact. Same hardcoding guardrail: apply 0.5 as a literal in prober.py before invoking Fairlearn; never let Fairlearn's thresholding logic choose the decision threshold for you.
dev_plans.metric: Fairlearn MetricFrame true_positive_rate difference (max-min across ethnicity groups) at fixed 0.5 threshold, computed once per epoch on the validation set
